businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue hit $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 2025 gross margin reached 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD transitioned to a $99/month subscription model. Key 2026 catalysts include Cybercab production starting April, Roadster reveal, and Terafab chip factory.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: vs. S&P 500 avg ~23x and auto sector avg ~12x. Extreme premium reflecting future growth expectations in robotaxis, energy, and AI

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026E EPS of $2.08. Over 4x the growth stock average of 30-40x. Prices in explosive robotaxi and energy growth

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 revenue $94.8B grew just 1% YoY. FY2026 expected to accelerate to +15% ($108.9B)

keyMetrics.name: Q1 2026 Delivery Consensus
keyMetrics.interpretation: 23-analyst consensus. +8% YoY but -13% QoQ from Q4's 418,227. Delivery report expected early next week

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: Flat over the weekend at $361.83. YTD -20%. Next week's jobs report and Q1 deliveries will set direction

keyMetrics.name: RSI (14-day)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Near the 30 oversold threshold. CCI at -151.82 and Williams %R at -95.73 confirm extreme selling pressure. Technical bounce increasingly possible

growthDrivers.title: Tesla captures 50%+ EV market share as overall EV sales plunge 28% (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: Despite a brutal 28% decline in overall EV sales, Tesla expanded its market share to over 50%. Brand power and Supercharger infrastructure proving to be decisive advantages during downturns

growthDrivers.title: SpaceX IPO rumors, potential Tesla valuation catalyst (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Growing speculation that SpaceX is preparing for an IPO. Could serve as an indirect catalyst for Tesla investors and trigger a broader Musk ecosystem revaluation

growthDrivers.title: Optimus3 robot production video, summer production start (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla revealed detailed Optimus3 gear reduction and precision hand design. Targeting 1M unit production rate by year-end 2026. Trefis values the robotics business at $2-3T

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April production + Roadster late April reveal
growthDrivers.description: Steerless, fully autonomous robotaxi Cybercab starts production alongside next-gen Roadster reveal. Two major catalysts concentrated in a single month

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $25B AI chip factory, Wedbush calls it 'game changer'
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush reaffirmed $600 target, calling Terafab semiconductor factory plans a 'game changer.' AI5 chip low-volume production starts late 2026

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA escalates FSD investigation (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: NHTSA upgraded its probe into FSD performance in low-visibility conditions. Trefis warned this could 'shatter the $1 trillion AI thesis'

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles FSD data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab AI chip vertical integration

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD miles. Scale of autonomous driving training data impossible for competitors to replicate quickly
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and others use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all in-house. Cost advantage and rapid innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fanbase generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China's #1, 100%+ growth in Europe. Aggressive pricing puts pressure on Tesla

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi service in SF, Las Vegas. Austin and Miami pilots started. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Leads in autonomous ride-hailing commercialization with 200K+ weekly paid rides

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: IONIQ, EV6 expanding global EV share. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: Musk challenges Twitter fraud verdict, claims jury's '$4.20 joke' shows bias
recentNews.significance: Musk's lawyer argues the jury mocked him by inserting a '$4.20 joke' into the verdict form, seeking a new trial. Legal uncertainty extends, raising management distraction concerns

recentNews.headline: CNBC: Nonfarm payrolls to determine market direction. Citigroup cuts US equity exposure
recentNews.significance: Citigroup reduced US equity allocation amid prolonged Iran-Israel conflict fears. March jobs report (4/3) becomes the key variable for recession risk assessment

recentNews.headline: HSBC maintains $131 target, warns of 65% further downside
recentNews.significance: HSBC maintains Reduce rating, representing the most extreme bear case. Continues to weigh on sentiment

recentNews.headline: Tesla captures 50%+ market share in brutal 28% EV sales slump
recentNews.significance: Tesla expanded share even as the overall EV market collapsed. Brand and infrastructure advantages prove decisive in downturns

recentNews.headline: Q1 2026 delivery consensus at 365,645 vehicles
recentNews.significance: 23-analyst average. +8% YoY but -13% QoQ. Actual delivery numbers expected early next week

analystOpinions.notableComment: 43 analysts: 15 Strong Buy, 2 Buy, 17 Hold, 9 Strong Sell. Wedbush maintains $600 high, HSBC $131 low. Next week's jobs data and Q1 deliveries will sharpen the bull/bear divide

risks.title: Below both 50-day and 200-day moving averages, accelerating downtrend
risks.description: Trading below 50-day SMA ($387) and 200-day SMA ($400). RSI at 33 near oversold but no trend reversal signal. Five consecutive declining sessions before the weekend

risks.title: Extreme valuation risk
risks.description: TTM P/E of 335x is 14.5x the S&P 500 average of 23x. Any growth disappointment could trigger sharp declines. Forward P/E of 174x is 4x+ the growth stock average

risks.title: Macro headwinds intensifying next week
risks.description: Citigroup cutting US equity exposure. Nonfarm payrolls (4/3) and prolonged Iran-Israel conflict could keep pressure on tech stocks

risks.title: Q1 delivery concerns + Musk legal distraction
risks.description: Consensus 365,645 units, -13% QoQ. Musk's Twitter fraud retrial request extends legal uncertainty and raises management focus concerns

risks.title: Bull defection crisis
risks.description: Former Tesla bull Trip Chowdhry switched to Sell with $150 target. Symbolic event signaling sentiment deterioration

risks.title: Musk risk (brand damage)
risks.description: DOGE political activities causing European sales decline. Ongoing insider selling. Brand damage directly impacting demand

buyReasons.title: Market share expansion during EV market collapse
buyReasons.rationale: Captured 50%+ share even as overall EV sales plunged 28%. Strong players get stronger in downturns. Biggest beneficiary when market recovers

buyReasons.title: April catalyst concentration (Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 earnings 4/21)
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, FSD Europe approval, and Q1 earnings all concentrated in April. Multiple chances for momentum reversal

buyReasons.title: RSI 33 technical oversold entry
buyReasons.rationale: RSI near the 30 oversold threshold. 27% discount from 52-week high. Technical bounce probability rising, though trend reversal confirmation needed

buyReasons.title: SpaceX IPO + Optimus robot, long-term rerating catalysts
buyReasons.rationale: SpaceX IPO rumors and Optimus3 production could justify Tesla's 'beyond auto company' valuation as long-term catalysts

overallOpinion: Next week is the pivotal moment. Nonfarm payrolls (4/3) and Q1 delivery numbers arrive simultaneously. Citigroup has cut US equity exposure and the Iran conflict continues to weigh on markets

Musk is seeking a retrial in the Twitter fraud case, claiming the jury's '$4.20 joke' proves bias. Legal uncertainty is extending, adding to management distraction. Bears like Trip Chowdhry ($150) and HSBC ($131) remain vocal
Still, capturing 50%+ EV market share during a 28% market collapse proves genuine competitive advantage. April catalysts (Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe) are waiting in the wings
$361.83, RSI 33 oversold. Below both 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400). No weekend price movement. Hold, wait for next week's data before acting

investmentVerdict.summary: RSI 33 oversold and April catalyst concentration provide bounce potential, but intensifying macro headwinds (Citigroup cutting exposure, geopolitical risk) and Musk's extending legal battles add uncertainty. Q1 deliveries and jobs data next week are the critical variables. Hold is rational; new entries safer after $340 support confirmation

threads.hook: TSLA: Oversold weekend, next week's jobs and deliveries will decide everything
threads.text:
$TSLA $361.83 (weekend, Friday close held)

RSI 33 oversold heading into the weekend. Next week is critical. Nonfarm payrolls (4/3) and Q1 deliveries arrive simultaneously, and Citigroup just cut US equity exposure.

Musk is fighting for a retrial in the Twitter fraud case. But Tesla captured 50%+ EV market share during a brutal 28% market decline.

Below 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400). Hold. Wait for April catalysts.

witty.hook: Tesla: Oversold, Musk in court over a $4.20 joke, and next week decides it all
witty.text:
$TSLA $361.83 (weekend)

RSI 33 oversold. The selling has been relentless. Meanwhile Musk is in court arguing the jury's $4.20 joke is evidence of bias. Shareholders are thinking 'maybe focus on the stock price.'

EV market down 28% but Tesla at 50%+ share. When everyone falls, the last one standing wins. Though right now, the winner's stock is falling too.

Next week: jobs data, deliveries, and the direction gets set. Hold.

empathy.hook: TSLA shareholders, the anxiety is real this weekend. Next week will provide clarity.
empathy.text:
$TSLA $361.83, RSI 33. Five straight declining sessions before the weekend.

Down 27% from highs, YTD -20%. It's natural to feel anxious. Citigroup cutting equity exposure doesn't help either.

But 50%+ EV market share during a 28% collapse is Tesla's real strength showing. Next week brings nonfarm payrolls and Q1 deliveries, the data that will set direction.

April has Cybercab, Roadster, and earnings (4/21) waiting. Target $408 (+12.8%). Now is the time for patience, not panic.
